next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 9

Delroy lay under the sheet at dawn and kept his eyes shut while Tiana slipped back into the apartment.

The side gate gave one soft click under her hands, then came her careful steps past the window unit, past Micah's sprawled legs, past the place on the floor where the linoleum bubbled up like old skin. Delroy knew the sound of each person in that room. Marcia's steps landed with purpose even when she was tired. Micah's dragged when he thought nobody was watching. Tiana, these days, moved like the house was a test she meant to pass without touching anything.

He stayed there, hot already, and listened to his daughter set her bag down without a word. He did not ask where she had been. He had no righteous questions left in him. He had used up the right to demand honest answers from anybody in that room.

By seven the apartment had its usual morning smell of bleach, old grease, damp towel, and heat beginning again. Marcia had on one of her house dresses and a scarf tied back from her face, and the sight of her wiping the counter made him feel accused in a way shouting almost never managed. She had a way of cleaning when she was furious that made every object in the room look guilty.

Micah was still under the fan with one foot out from the sheet. Tiana sat at the table with a mug of bush tea and her phone face down beside it. She had changed clothes, but her eyes still looked used, and not from crying. Delroy saw the same shirt from yesterday balled in the laundry bucket. He looked away before his mind started arranging things he did not want to know about his ch...
open_hooks_to_carry: Delroy's confession comes from shame, not villain caricature.
Marcia's fury is about being cut out of the household math as much as the missing money.
prev_tail_excerpt:
...by digging another under you."

Micah's mouth tightened, but he did not argue again. He dropped onto the bed by the wall and tugged one sneaker loose with the heel of the other, not taking his eyes off the floor.

Delroy pulled a mug from the cabinet and held it without doing anything else. "Give me till Monday. I'll sort something."

The sentence sat in the middle of the room unanswered.

Marcia knew that voice. Delroy used it when he wanted possibility to sound already half done. He could make bad news hold still for a few minutes by talking warm enough around it. Usually that worked best on people who loved him and did not have a bill in their hand.

"Don't promise into the air before the girl even comes home," Marcia said.

Delroy frowned. "What you want me to say then?"

"The truth. When Tiana walks in here, nobody tells her church is cooking up mercy. Nobody spends tomorrow before it gets here."

Micah finally looked up. "You calling her?"

"No. I'm not telling her on a sidewalk or in somebody else's break room."

Finally Micah said, very low, "So we really don't have no backup."

Marcia pulled the church handkerchief from her bag, then the notebook, and set them beside the envelopes. Sweat and powder had turned one corner of the cloth gray.

"No," she said.

Delroy stayed by the sink with the empty mug in his hand. Micah sat on the bed with one sneaker on and one off, his phone face-down beside him. Marcia opened the notebook to the page with her name and listened for the side gate. No one said Tiana's name again. In the room, nobody moved. They all listened for it.
state_deltas_required: Force Delroy to explain the document lie and get a plan strong enough to hold.
Tiana leaves for the night with Malik, and their first sex deepens attachment while exposing how badly she wants a body she can treat as air.
